_doc: Manifest of vendored external libraries. See planning/osc-mixer-foundation/05-vendoring-strategy.md for the workflow. Updated by tools/vendor.py.

OSCAudio.purpose: OSC dispatch helpers (OSCUtils, isMine/isTarget), OSC<Class> Audio Library wrappers, OSCSubscribe machinery, /teensy*/audio/ debug surface
contributionTargets: F32 wrapper extensions for OpenAudio classes

OpenAudio_ArduinoLibrary.purpose: F32 (32-bit float) audio path for the entire mixer signal graph; provides AudioMixer4_F32, AudioFilterBiquad_F32, AudioInputUSB_F32, AudioOutputUSB_F32, etc.

localPatches.description: Add setPeakingEq(stage, frequency, gain, q) to AudioFilterBiquad_F32.h. Completes the RBJ cookbook convenience set by adding peaking (parametric) EQ — the most common EQ type in professional channel strips and the missing piece alongside setLowpass / setHighpass / setBandpass / setNotch / setLowShelf / setHighShelf. Needed for TDspMixer's /ch/NN/eq/B/type 'peq' value so SignalGraphBinding can configure the band with one call instead of hand-computing coefficients and using setCoefficients() directly. Math verified against musicdsp.org/files/Audio-EQ-Cookbook.txt peakingEQ section; unity case (gain=0 dB) reduces to H(z)=1 at all frequencies.

contributionTargets: Add top-level LICENSE file (MIT terms currently in source headers only)
Upstream PR for setPeakingEq (applied locally; needs fork remote + PR before upstreamFork can be set)

Audio.purpose: Stock Teensy Audio Library; vendored to allow pinning to specific commits and applying uncommitted PRs (notably multi-channel USB extensions). Used via lib_ignore=Audio in platformio.ini so PlatformIO uses our copy instead of the framework-bundled one.
contributionTargets: Multi-channel USB audio extensions to AudioInputUSB / AudioOutputUSB (after Spike 2)

CNMAT_OSC.purpose: Upstream CNMAT/OSC. Vendored because the framework-bundled Teensyduino fork is older than OSCAudio expects (missing 2-arg getString, getBlobLength, etc.). Library directory name is 'OSC' per library.properties; PlatformIO references it as such. Used via lib_ignore=OSC in platformio.ini so PlatformIO uses our copy.

teensy_cores.purpose: Teensy 4.x core source. Vendored to enable modifications to USB descriptors (multi-channel USB) and USB audio buffers/feature units (Windows recording slider). Overlaid onto PlatformIO framework cache via tools/cores_overlay.py before each compile.

localPatches.description: Add #include <type_traits> to cores/teensy4/IntervalTimer.h. The upstream file uses std::is_arithmetic_v, std::is_integral_v, std::is_floating_point_v, and `if constexpr` (all C++17 features) but does not include the <type_traits> header that declares them. The file only compiles when something else in the include chain transitively pulls type_traits in first. PlatformIO's standalone compile of IntervalTimer.cpp does not get that lucky transitive include and fails to find the symbols. The patch is a minimal one-line include addition. Should be PR'd to PaulStoffregen/cores as an upstream completeness fix unrelated to T-DSP's specific use case.

contributionTargets: PR upstream the IntervalTimer.h <type_traits> include (real upstream completeness bug)
USB Audio Class feature unit on input terminal for Windows recording slider (Spike 3 outcome)
